NCT01465763.name: OCTAVE Induction 1
NCT01465763.group: Moderate-severe active UC, tofacitinib 10 mg BID vs placebo induction 8 wk

allOutcomes.title: Clinical remission at week 8 (Mayo score <=2, no subscore >1)

NCT01465763.snippet: Source: Sandborn WJ et al. NEJM 2017;376:1723-1736 (OCTAVE Induction 1).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: 598 adults with moderate-to-severe active UC (total Mayo 6-12, endoscopic subscore >=2) who had failed conventional therapy or a TNF antagonist were randomized 4:1 to tofacitinib 10 mg BID (n=476) or placebo (n=122) for 8 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary — Clinical Remission at Week 8
evidence.text: Clinical remission: 88/476 (18.5%) tofacitinib vs 10/122 (8.2%) placebo. Treatment effect 10.3 percentage points (95% CI 4.2-16.4; P=0.007). Endoscopic improvement 31.3% vs 15.6%.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Subject must be at least 18 years of age.~* Males and females with a documented diagnosis of UC at least 4 months prior to entry into the study.~* Subjects with moderately to severely active UC based on Mayo score criteria.~* Subjects must have failed or be intolerant of at least one of the following treatments for UC:~  * Corticosteroids (oral or intravenous).~ * Azathioprine or 6 mercaptopurine (6 MP).~ * Anti TNF-alpha therapy.~Exclusion Criteria:~* Presence of indeterm...

NCT02819635.name: U-ACHIEVE Induction
NCT02819635.group: Moderate-severe UC, upadacitinib 45 mg QD vs placebo 8 wk

allOutcomes.title: Clinical remission at week 8 (Adapted Mayo score)

NCT02819635.snippet: Source: Danese S et al. Lancet 2022;399:2113-2128 (U-ACHIEVE Induction).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: 473 adults with moderately-to-severely active UC were randomized 2:1 to upadacitinib 45 mg once daily (n=319) or placebo (n=154) for 8-week induction.

evidence.label: Primary — Clinical Remission at Week 8
evidence.text: Clinical remission (Adapted Mayo Score): 26% (83/319) upadacitinib vs 5% (7/154) placebo. Adjusted treatment difference 22.3 percentage points (95% CI 15.7-28.8; P<0.0001). All ranked secondary endpoints positive.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 16 Years; max age 75 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~Note: Adolescent participants who are 16 or 17 years old will be eligible to participate if approved by the country or regulatory/health authority. If approval has not been granted, only participants ≥18 years old will be enrolled. Adolescents must weigh ≥ 40 kilograms and meet the definition of Tanner Stage 5 at Screening Visit.~* Diagnosis of ulcerative colitis for 90 days or greater prior to Baseline, confirmed by colonoscopy during the Screening Period, with exclusion of...

NCT02914522.name: SELECTION Induction A
NCT02914522.group: Moderate-severe UC biologic-naive (Induction A only), filgotinib 200 mg QD vs placebo 10 wk. Feagan 2021 Lancet ran two parallel induction cohorts (A=biologic-naive, B=biologic-experienced); cN=98 here is Induction-A-naive placebo arm only — pooled-A+B placebo of n=137 reported elsewhere does NOT apply.
NCT02914522.snippet: Source: Feagan BG et al. Lancet 2021;397:2372-2384 (SELECTION).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: Induction Study A: 659 biologic-naive adults with moderate-to-severe UC randomized 2:2:1 to filgotinib 200 mg (n=245) or 100 mg or placebo (n=98, ITT shown), 10 weeks. Filgotinib 200 mg primary focus.

evidence.label: Primary — Clinical Remission at Week 10
evidence.text: Clinical remission filgotinib 200 mg: 66/245 (26.1%) vs placebo 15/98 (15.3%). Treatment difference 10.8 percentage points (95% CI 2.1-19.5; P=0.0157). Biologic-experienced stratum showed similar but smaller effect.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 75 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Males or non-pregnant, non-lactating females, ages 18 to 75 years, inclusive based on the date of the screening visit~* Documented diagnosis of UC of at least 6 months AND with a minimum disease extent of 15 cm from the anal verge.
